content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Reviewed the new success-reconciliation flow end-to-end, including `git diff`, `success_reconciliation.rs`, the daemon-loop integration, and the milestone persistence changes. I found two correctness gaps: milestone-update failures are still finalized as completed tasks with no repair path, and the replay path can falsely fail an already-synced reconciliation because it has no durable notion of whether the post-close flush already succeeded.

## Amendments

1. In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1787-1813`, do not swallow `ReconciliationError::MilestoneUpdateFailed`. `reconcile_success` closes and flushes the bead before it calls `update_milestone_state` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:141-158`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:242-286`), but the daemon currently only logs that failure and then both success paths still mark the daemon task completed (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:966-967`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1523-1525`). Since completed tasks are terminal and `retry_task` only accepts `Failed` or `Aborted` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:373-399`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:453-460`), any transient snapshot/journal/lineage write failure permanently loses the milestone progress update, completion journal entry, and durable task-to-bead linkage.
   Rationale: This leaves the bead closed in `br` while the milestone state stays stale forever, which violates the acceptance criteria for progress advancement and completion recording.
2. The replay path is still not fully crash-idempotent. `reconcile_success` always re-runs `br sync --flush-only` even when the bead is already closed (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:141-145`), and `try_reconcile_success` treats any replay-time `BrSyncFailed` as a hard reconciliation failure (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1774-1785`). Each replay constructs a fresh `BrMutationAdapter` with `has_unsync_mutations` reset to false (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1735-1740`, `src/adapters/br_process.rs:475-479`), so the code has no durable way to distinguish "close succeeded but flush never landed" from "close and flush already succeeded before the crash". A transient `br` outage after a fully successful first reconciliation can therefore incorrectly move an already-reconciled task into the failed/needs-operator path on replay.
   Rationale: The implementation correctly covers the crash window between `br close` and `br sync`, but it mishandles the equally real crash window after a successful sync and before `mark_completed`, so the end-to-end replay behavior is not truly idempotent.
